steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" dialog.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click on the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface at coordinates (288, 101).
Click the "Default folder" field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".
Click the "Run and Debug (Ctrl+Shift+D)" option in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT sidebar of Visual Studio Code, located directly under the selected account section.
Click the "Debug" button in the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code to start debugging in Microsoft 365 Agents Playground.

Click the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option in the Run and Debug dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code interface, indicated by the highlighted menu item and the red crosshair at position (215, 141).
Click the "Start Debugging" (F5) button in the Run and Debug panel dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Add" button in the app information dialog for vscuse_app_yqrdnlocal within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Open" button in the "Added successfully!" dialog for the app "vscuse_app_yqrdnlocal" within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Type a message" input box in the Microsoft Teams web application chat interface to activate the message composer for the channel "vscuse_app_yqrdnlocal."
Type the text  ${{var:test_data:test_######}} into the message input box at the bottom of a Microsoft Teams chat interface within the 'vscuse_app_yqrdnlocal' chat thread in a Chrome browser window.
Press the Enter key to send the message "test" from the chat input box in the Microsoft Teams web application chat interface.
